scope.summary: Implement a base-color recolor pass in the PBR export pipeline that applies a hue/saturation/value transform to the albedo map at export time, producing a recolored colorway without modifying the source TailorPbrMaterial authority row
acceptance_criteria: tailor_generate_pbr_maps accepts an optional ColorwayOverride struct with target_hue_deg, saturation_scale, and value_scale fields
When ColorwayOverride is provided, the exported albedo map has hue shifted to target_hue_deg while preserving luminance variation from the source texture
The recolor is applied as a CPU-side HSV transform on the source base color image using the image crate; it MUST NOT modify the tailor_pbr_materials row
The TailorPbrMapsGenerated EventLedger event payload includes colorway_override: Option<ColorwayOverride> so colorway variants are distinguishable in the audit trail
A model can call tailor_generate_pbr_maps with a blue colorway override on a red-based fabric texture and verify the exported albedo map artifact is blue-dominant
scope.risk_if_missed: Without recolor-on-export, producing color variants of a garment (red dress, blue dress, black dress) requires authoring a separate TailorPbrMaterial for each color. This multiplies texture storage and authoring effort proportionally to the colorway count.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
